---
title: Roll your own Neovim statusline
date: 2026-04-15
---

The default Neovim statusline looks like this:

![Default Neovim statusline](nvim-statusline-default.png)

A zero-config setup gets you:

*   The path to the current file (`~/Repos/dotfiles/.config/nvim/init.lua`)
*   A modified status indicator (`[+]` to show there are unsaved changes)
*   The cursor position (`49,29`)
*   The position of the viewport (`62%`)
*   As of 0.12 you also get other stuff sometimes, e.g. LSP progress messages

For my money this is actually a great set of defaults. But, I spend hours every
day in this editor, so a few tweaks will go a long way. I want my statusline to
be:

*   Fancy but still understated
*   Simple (no big plugin dependencies)
*   Hackable, e.g. in case I want to see other information in different contexts

This turns out to be very achievable with nvim's built-in functionality! The
following is a rough guide.
